summary: All three round-1 major findings are resolved. isWarmupInProgress_emitsFalse_afterCompletion now uses a CompletableDeferred gate and asserts both the true-while-in-flight and false-after-release transitions, so an implementation that never flips the flag would fail. The old cancellation test has been replaced with isWarmupInProgress_staysTrue_acrossCancelAndRestart_andClearsOnCompletion + isWarmupInProgress_emitsFalse_afterBareCancellation — together these pin down the no-flicker contract (stays true across handover, clears via finally on bare cancel). The coordinator scope now uses StandardTestDispatcher(testScheduler) matching the task's Implementation Hint. The new flicker-guard test genuinely exercises the identity-check in the production finally block: if the guard (`if (warmupJob === coroutineContext[Job])`) were removed, the first Job's finally would set the flag to false during cancelAndJoin; after advanceUntilIdle the second Job would still be suspended on secondRelease.await(), so the assertTrue would fail with value=false. advanceUntilIdle is sufficient to drive cancelAndJoin through because the coordinator scope now runs on the same testScheduler. Round-1 minors are also addressed: false-assert added for the both-empty case, both edge-case tests added, subscriptionCount==0 proves observer self-cancels, and fake ModelRegistry defaults are softened to success/no-op. The ErrorLog sleep-based polling remains (Thread.sleep(20) in awaitLogFile) but is bounded to 2 s and the Round-1 report already marked it minor — not re-flagging.
